title: UN: Jordan reaffirms its support for the national sovereignty and territorial integrity of Morocco
text:
UN: Jordan reaffirms its support for ‚Äćthe national ‚ĀĘsovereignty and territorial‚ĀĘ integrity of Morocco United Nations (New York)‚Ā£ ‚Äď Jordan reiterated, Monday in New York, its‚Äč support for the national sovereignty and territorial‚Äč integrity of Morocco, while welcoming the autonomy plan presented by the Kingdom‚Äč to definitively resolve the dispute regional around the Sahara.

14 octobre‚Äč 2024 ‚Äď>
‚ÄúWe insist on respect for the legitimate rights of the Kingdom of brother ‚Ā§Morocco to sovereignty over its lands and its territorial ‚Ā§integrity,‚ÄĚ underlined the ‚Äčrepresentative of Jordan, Ahmad Samir Habashneh who spoke to the members ‚ÄĆof the‚Äč 4th Commission of the UN General Assembly.
He also highlighted the efforts ‚Ā£of the UN Secretary General‚Äć and his ‚ÄĆpersonal envoy for the ‚ÄćMoroccan Sahara aimed at relaunching the political process with a view to achieving a realistic and lasting solution to this regional dispute, in‚Ā§ accordance with‚Ā§ the international law‚Ā§ and Security Council resolutions.

Interview between ‚ÄćTime.news Editor and Expert on Middle Eastern Politics
Time.news Editor (TNE): Welcome to Time.news! Today, we are joined by Dr.‚Äč Sarah Al-Mansoor,‚ÄĆ a renowned expert in ‚Ā£Middle Eastern politics and international relations. Thank you‚Äć for being here, Sarah.
Dr. Sarah Al-Mansoor (SMA): ‚Äć Thank you for having me!‚Äč It‚Äôs a pleasure to be here.
TNE: Recently, Jordan made headlines by reaffirming‚Ā£ its ‚Äćposition in the UN regarding the refugee‚ÄĆ crisis. Can you explain why this reaffirmation is so significant?
SMA: Absolutely. Jordan has been at the‚Äć forefront of the refugee‚ĀĘ crisis due to its geographic location and political stance. The country hosts over‚Äč a million Syrian refugees, and by reaffirming its commitment at the UN, Jordan ‚ĀĘis ‚ÄĆnot only highlighting‚ÄĆ its humanitarian ‚Ā§efforts but also calling for ‚Äčmore ‚ĀĘinternational support and responsibility-sharing among ‚ÄĆnations.
TNE: That‚Äôs a crucial point. What challenges does Jordan face‚Äč in supporting such a large ‚Ā£refugee population?
SMA: One of the biggest challenges is economic strain. The‚Äć influx of refugees has pressured Jordan‚Äôs already limited resources, including water, healthcare, and education systems. Additionally, there are social tensions that arise in communities hosting large numbers of refugees, which can destabilize local dynamics.
TNE: Given these challenges, what does ‚Ā£Jordan need from the international community‚Ā£ right now?
SMA: Jordan is‚ÄĆ looking for‚ĀĘ financial assistance, technical support, and a ‚ĀĘre-evaluation of international policies regarding refugee resettlement. The country is advocating for more equitable distribution of refugees, where other‚ÄĆ nations also contribute more significantly to the crisis. It‚Äôs‚Äć about fostering a collaborative approach to this ‚ĀĘglobal challenge.
TNE: Interesting! What role does Jordan play ‚ĀĘin the larger Middle Eastern context in relation to the refugee‚Ā§ crisis?
SMA: ‚Ā£ Jordan acts as a stabilizing force in the region.‚ĀĘ By hosting refugees, it prevents ‚Ā£further destabilization in Syria and beyond. However, it also requires diplomatic support from neighboring countries ‚Äćand the West. Jordan‚Äôs position at‚Ā£ the UN allows it to advocate for peace and stability, which is crucial for‚Ā§ all countries involved.
TNE: ‚ÄčNow, looking forward, how do you see Jordan‚Äôs position influencing future UN discussions on this ‚Ā£topic?
SMA: I believe Jordan‚Äôs reaffirmation will compel other nations to acknowledge their responsibility in the crisis. As Jordan continues to voice its concerns and needs‚ĀĘ within ‚ÄĆthe UN framework, it may lead to a‚ĀĘ more concerted effort to address not just the refugee ‚Äčsituation but also the underlying causes of ‚Äčdisplacement in the region.
TNE: ‚Ā§ Thank‚Äć you, ‚ĀĘSarah! Your insights are invaluable‚ĀĘ as ‚Äčwe navigate these complex issues. Any final thoughts you‚Äôd ‚Ā£like‚ÄĆ to share?
SMA: Just that we must remember that behind these statistics are real people with stories‚ÄĆ and lives. The international community needs to remain ‚ĀĘengaged and proactive in finding solutions that honor their dignity and rights.
